data.title: Close residual-state blind spot in brainstorm and preplanning review pipeline
data.description:
## Context

When an epic involves moving, deprecating, or renaming file paths, the planning pipeline's review gates fail to detect that existing codebase references to the old state become stale. This allowed epic 9b59-bac7 (plugin boundary enforcement) to pass all review gates while leaving ~567 hardcoded path references unaddressed, forcing a corrective second epic. The blind spot exists at four independent layers (Part B self-review, Part C consumer scan, preplanning red/blue team adversarial review, and brainstorm scenario analysis) that should provide defense-in-depth but all share the same gap: they only activate on explicit file modifications, not on implied path deprecations. Additionally, when adversarial review or scenario analysis discovers completeness gaps in the epic's success criteria, findings are appended as informational comments rather than triggering SC revision — allowing the gap to pass through the approval gate unaddressed.

## Success Criteria

- Part B of the epic scrutiny pipeline includes a "residual state" probe that forces a codebase grep when the epic involves path deprecation, relocation, or rename — verified by a test simulating a "move files from A to B" epic
- Part C's trigger condition activates on SCs that reference moving, deprecating, removing, or renaming a file or directory — not only on SCs that reference modifying a file consumed by 2+ others
- The preplanning red team taxonomy includes a "Residual References" category and an escalate_to_epic finding type; blue-team-filter.md is updated to recognize and triage escalate_to_epic findings. When the orchestrator applies an escalate_to_epic finding, it appends a new SC to the epic description via ticket edit and emits REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm — verified by asserting the epic description contains the new SC text, not that a comment exists
- Brainstorm scenario analysis findings that expose gaps in the SCs trigger SC revision. The pipeline halts and presents revised SCs to the user via AskUserQuestion for re-approval before proceeding — including in replan paths where the approval gate was previously passed. Verified by a test exercising the replan-triggered re-approval flow
- Behavioral test with RED baseline: first confirm unmodified pipeline does NOT trigger a residual-state finding for a "move from A to B" epic; then confirm modified pipeline triggers Part B probe + Part C scan + RESIDUAL_REFERENCES finding in review output
- The brainstorm-preplanning replan cascade is bounded by sprint.max_replan_cycles — escalate_to_epic then REPLAN_ESCALATE then SC revision cannot exceed the configured cycle limit

## Dependencies

Soft dependency on stability of REPLAN_ESCALATE contract and sprint replan cycle handling. Coordinate with 9b59-bac7 and 3601-f085 to avoid concurrent modifications to brainstorm SKILL.md, preplanning SKILL.md, and epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md. This epic should land after those epics reach stable state on the shared skill files.

## Approach

Targeted probe and trigger expansion at each pipeline layer, plus finding type uplift so red team and scenario findings propagate back to epic SCs when they identify completeness gaps.

## Scenario Analysis

- Infinite replan loop (critical): escalate_to_epic → REPLAN_ESCALATE → SC revision could loop unboundedly if SC revision partially addresses but does not fully close the gap. Mitigated by SC6 (cycle bound).
- SC re-approval in replan path (high): SC revision after prior approval could silently invalidate user-approved SCs. Mitigated by SC4 (mandatory re-approval via AskUserQuestion).
- Blue team taxonomy gap (high): escalate_to_epic unknown to blue team filter. Mitigated by SC3 (blue-team-filter.md update).
- CHECKPOINT backward compatibility (high): old finding labels in resumed sessions may misroute. Consideration for implementation.
- Sub-agent context propagation (high): REPLAN_ESCALATE emitted from sub-agent context may not propagate. Consideration for implementation.
